steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))

discordance.evidence: Alanine Aminotransferase (ALT): 678.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-40.0); Asparate Aminotransferase (AST): 989.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-40.0); Lactate Dehydrogenase (LD): 934.0 IU/L (ref 94.0-250.0); Lipase: 28.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)

alarm_detect.summary: Massively elevated transaminases (AST ~25× ULN, ALT ~17× ULN) with markedly elevated LDH in a patient whose epigastric/back pain presentation and normal lipase do not suggest pancreatitis — this degree of hepatocellular injury is strikingly out of proportion to the clinical picture and demands urgent further evaluation (e.g., hepatic imaging, viral hepatitis serologies, ischemic hepatitis workup) before defaulting to a routine biliary or GI pathway.

alarm_resolve.reason: The ordered hepatobiliary ultrasound is the appropriate first-line imaging modality to evaluate massively elevated transaminases and the physician's reasoning explicitly targets the discordance between the hepatocellular injury pattern and the clinical presentation, seeking to distinguish biliary obstruction from parenchymal disease.

study_inadequacy.evidence: portions of the pancreatic tail obscured by overlying bowel gas
study_inadequacy.note: The pancreas was not fully visualized on ultrasound, leaving the pancreatic tail unevaluated

discordance.evidence: Alanine Aminotransferase (ALT): 678.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-40.0); Asparate Aminotransferase (AST): 989.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-40.0); Lactate Dehydrogenase (LD): 934.0 IU/L (ref 94.0-250.0); The gallbladder is mildly distended with multiple gallstones. There is a small sliver of pericholecystic fluid. No definitive sonographic ___ sign was elicited.

alarm_detect.summary: Massively elevated transaminases (AST 989, ALT 678, LDH 934) are grossly out of proportion to the ultrasound findings of uncomplicated cholelithiasis with only a small sliver of pericholecystic fluid and no Murphy's sign, and the pancreatic tail was not visualized on ultrasound, leaving both the cause of the hepatocellular injury and a pancreatic pathology unexplained.

alarm_resolve.reason: MRCP is capable of visualizing the full biliary tree including the distal CBD and pancreatic tail obscured on ultrasound, directly addressing both the study inadequacy (unevaluated pancreatic tail) and the discordance (massively elevated transaminases unexplained by uncomplicated cholelithiasis), and the physician's reasoning explicitly targets both the biliary obstruction question and the bowel-gas-obscured distal biliary tree/pancreatic tail region.
